api/types.purpose: Holds manually-authored Go types that fill gaps where oapi-codegen cannot generate correct types from the OpenAPI spec (e.g. oneOf/anyOf schemas). Types here are referenced from the OpenAPI spec via `x-go-type` so generated clients use them correctly — this package must never contain business logic.

patterns.name: x-go-type reference only
patterns.description: New types are added here only when oapi-codegen cannot generate them (oneOf/anyOf). The type is then referenced in the OpenAPI spec via the `x-go-type` extension, not imported directly by hand-written code.

patterns.name: Pure type definitions
patterns.description: Files in this package contain only type declarations (struct, interface, type alias). No functions, no methods, no init(), no business logic.

key_file_guides.role: Package declaration and authoritative comment explaining when and why to add types here — read before adding any new type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The comment is the contract: only add types for spec constructs oapi-codegen cannot handle (oneOf/anyOf). If oapi-codegen can handle it, do not add it here.

anti_patterns: Adding types that oapi-codegen can generate automatically from the OpenAPI spec
Adding business logic, helper functions, or methods to types in this package
Duplicating types already present in api/api.gen.go or api/v3/api.gen.go
Importing this package from domain code (openmeter/*) — it is consumed via x-go-type by generated code only
Editing api/api.gen.go or api/v3/api.gen.go to inline types instead of using this package

decisions.decision: Types live in a separate package rather than inline in generated files
decisions.rationale: Generated files (api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go) must not be hand-edited; a separate package allows manual type definitions to survive regeneration cycles while remaining importable by generated code via x-go-type references.
